title: salix × multidentata
intro: Salix × multidentata, also known as the crack willow, is a hybrid plant species of willow trees that is commonly found in Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its salicin content. The health benefits of Salix × multidentata include relief from pain, reduction of fever, and treatment of digestive issues, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and its extracts are also used in the production of herbal preparations, including salves, creams, and ointments.
